title: Neottopteris squamulata
intro: Neottopteris squamulata, also known as the mountain fern or scaly maidenhair fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties and is traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The bioactive constituents of Neottopteris squamulata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Neottopteris squamulata is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as being incorporated into salves and ointments to treat skin conditions.
taxonomy: Neottopteris squamulata is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is native to tropical Asia, including countries such as India, Malaysia, and Indonesia. This species is classified as an epiphytic fern, growing on other plants for support. Neottopteris squamulata is often found in tropical forests and is considered a relatively rare species. The classification of Neottopteris squamulata as a species of fern places it within the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, and class Polypodiopsida.
